topic: In China employee termination: position paper for foreign boards on legal defensibility and payroll risk
primary_keyword: China employee termination position paper foreign employers
related_keywords: China Labor Contract Law termination compliance
China severance and unlawful termination exposure

sources.title: Labor Contract Law of the People's Republic of China
sources.fit_note: Primary legal basis for termination routes and consequences.

sources.title: Regulations for the Implementation of the Labor Contract Law of the PRC (State Council Decree No. 535)
sources.fit_note: Provides implementation detail needed for board-level policy design.

sources.title: Interpretation of the Supreme People's Court on Issues Concerning the Application of Law in the Trial of Labor Dispute Cases (I)
sources.fit_note: Supports dispute-risk framing and evidentiary governance principles.

sources.title: Social Insurance Law of the People's Republic of China
sources.fit_note: Anchors statutory closure obligations tied to termination payroll handling.

sources.title: Employee Termination in China: A Guide for Employers
sources.fit_note: Provides practical translation of legal requirements for foreign operators.

source_replacement_notes: Rotated from role-relay structure to a board-facing position-paper format; source stack unchanged to preserve legal certainty.
Excluded weak-fit tax/comparative sources because this run focuses on legal defensibility strategy rather than calculation detail.
Kept China Briefing as practical implementation lens while prioritizing official law and judicial interpretation.
consensus: Termination in China should be governed as a legal risk decision, not only an HR operation.
Evidence and communication consistency are leading indicators of dispute outcomes.
Foreign employers benefit from board-level guardrails that force legal-first sequencing.
uncertainties: City-level handling practices and evidentiary emphasis differ.
Complex component treatment in final payments may require local confirmation.
Official English texts may not fully capture all Chinese-language updates in practice.
source_fit_assessment: Sufficient for a position-paper format because it combines binding legal authority with one high-fit practitioner source for foreign-reader operational interpretation.
